docs.title: Technical Writing Portfolio
docs.text:
Hi, I'm David Posto, a technical writer with experience creating knowledge base articles, SOPs, troubleshooting guides, and end-user support documentation for IT service desks and healthcare workflows.

This site is a collection of writing samples that demonstrate my documentation style: clear step-by-step procedures, consistent structure, and plain language designed for front-line support staff.

docs.title: What You'll Find Here
docs.text:
This portfolio contains sample documentation demonstrating:

  • Knowledge base article development
  • Step-by-step troubleshooting procedures
  • Process and workflow documentation
  • Decision-tree based support guides
  • Documentation written for front-line support teams

docs.title: About These Articles
docs.text:
These articles are based on real help desk workflows I've documented professionally. Names, locations, and organization-specific details have been generalized to protect confidentiality.

Each article follows a structured format:

  • Symptoms — what the caller or user is experiencing
  • Solution — step-by-step procedures with decision branches
  • Escalation — what to do when standard steps don't resolve the issue

docs.title: New Patient Scheduling
docs.text:
Healthcare workflow documentation describing the process for scheduling new patients, including decision points, insurance verification, provider selection, and escalation paths.

Read Article

docs.title: ISP Static Configuration
docs.text:
IT networking documentation describing the process for verifying and reconfiguring a back office machine to test ISP static IP settings, including adapter configuration, direct ISP device testing, and escalation paths.

Read Article

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I'm based in the Indianapolis area and am currently seeking Technical Writer, Knowledge Management, and IT Documentation opportunities.

  • GitHub: github.com/dposto

docs.title: Verifying ISP Static IP Configuration Using a Back Office Workstation
docs.text:
This article provides procedures for verifying whether a site's ISP-provided static IP configuration is correctly configured and reachable by directly connecting a workstation to the ISP equipment.

Author David Posto Document Type Knowledge Base Article Audience IT Support Agents Portfolio Version June 2026

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text:
Use this procedure when troubleshooting a site that may have an ISP-side static IP configuration issue.

This process may be required when:

  1. The site is operating on a backup internet connection.
  2. The site is connected through a secondary WAN interface.
  3. The site is completely offline.
  4. Connectivity issues suggest a static IP configuration problem.

docs.title: Pre-Step 1: Set Priority
docs.text: 1. Set the Service Order priority to Priority 1.

docs.title: Pre-Step 2: Verify Eligibility
docs.text:
1. Verify one of the following conditions exists:

  2. The site is already operating on a backup internet connection.

  3. The site is completely offline.

Note: In many environments, a secondary WAN interface is configured using DHCP and can be used for temporary connectivity testing.

docs.title: Pre-Step 3: Verify ISP Static IP Information
docs.text:
1. Ask the caller:

Do you have the site's static IP information from the ISP?

  1. Record the following information:

  2. Static IP Address

  3. Subnet Mask

  4. Default Gateway

  5. If the caller does not have the information:

  6. Inform the caller:

    You'll need to contact your ISP and request your static IP address, subnet mask, and default gateway information.

  7. Document the interaction.

  8. Close the Service Order.

docs.title: Step 1: Verify Secondary WAN Connectivity
docs.text:
1. Confirm the following:

  2. The ISP modem or router is connected to the secondary WAN interface.

  3. The secondary WAN connection is active.

  4. Have the caller perform an internet search to verify connectivity.

  5. If connectivity succeeds:

  6. Document successful connectivity.

  7. Proceed to Step 2.

  8. If connectivity fails:

  9. Recommend a temporary backup internet solution if appropriate.

  10. Document findings.
  11. Close the Service Order.

docs.title: Step 2: Identify the Workstation
docs.text:
1. Determine which workstation will be used for testing.

  2. If necessary:

  3. Identify the workstation's network connection.

  4. Verify the network port currently in use.
  5. Document the connection details.

  6. Ensure the workstation can be restored to its original state after testing.

  7. Proceed to Step 3.

docs.title: Step 3: Establish Remote Access
docs.text: 1. Initiate a screen-sharing session with the workstation if available.

docs.title: Step 4: Configure the Network Adapter
docs.text:
1. Open:

  2. View Network Connections

  3. Ethernet Adapter Properties

  4. Internet Protocol Version 4 (IPv4)

  5. Document the current configuration:

  6. IP Address

  7. Subnet Mask
  8. Default Gateway
  9. Preferred DNS Server

  10. Alternate DNS Server

  11. Update the adapter using the ISP-provided static IP information.

  12. Configure DNS servers as appropriate for the environment.

  13. Save the changes.

docs.title: Step 5: Connect the Workstation Directly to the ISP Device
docs.text:
1. Determine whether the ISP device is:

  2. An Optical Network Terminal (ONT)

  3. A modem or router with a single Ethernet port

  4. A modem or router with multiple available Ethernet ports

  5. Connect the workstation directly to the ISP equipment using the appropriate method.

⚠️ Warning: This process may temporarily interrupt network connectivity for the site.

docs.title: Step 6: Test Internet Access
docs.text:
1. Have the caller browse to:

https://speedtest.net

  1. If the test is successful:

  2. Record:

    1. Download Speed
    2. Upload Speed
    3. Proceed to Step 7.

  3. If the test is unsuccessful:

  4. Record:

    1. Exact error message
    2. Relevant troubleshooting observations
    3. Proceed to Step 7.

docs.title: Step 7: Restore Original Adapter Configuration
docs.text:
1. Return the network adapter settings to the values documented in Step 4.

  2. Save the changes.

docs.title: Step 8: Restore Network Connections
docs.text:
1. If any network connections were disconnected during testing:

  2. Reconnect the workstation to its original network connection.

  3. Reconnect the secondary WAN connection if applicable.
  4. Verify that network services have recovered.

docs.title: Step 9: Verify Business-Critical Applications
docs.text:
1. Confirm connectivity to business-critical applications and services.

  2. Document the results.

  3. If connectivity issues remain:

  4. Record the current public IP address.

  5. Escalate according to established procedures.

docs.title: Step 10: Escalation
docs.text:
1. If the issue remains unresolved:

  2. Document all troubleshooting performed.

  3. Include relevant test results and observations.
  4. Assign the Service Order according to established escalation procedures.

docs.title: New Patient Scheduling
docs.text:
This article provides procedures for scheduling new primary care patients, including insurance verification, provider selection, appointment scheduling, and escalation scenarios.

Author David Posto Document Type Knowledge Base Article Audience Scheduling Agents Last Updated June 2026

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text: New patient needs to schedule an appointment with a Primary Care Provider (PCP).

docs.title: Pre-Step 1: Cardiac Concerns or Shortness of Breath
docs.text:
1. If the patient at any time expresses concern with cardiac issues or shortness of breath:

    1. Inform the caller:

      Your symptoms suggest a serious condition that requires immediate medical attention.

      Please hang up the phone and call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room.

    2. Close the Service Order.

docs.title: Pre-Step 2: Addressing Sensitive Topics
docs.text: 1. For information on how to address sensitive topics during calls such as gender or diagnosis, proceed to Article 7392.

docs.title: Step 1: Determine if New or Existing Patient
docs.text:
1. Ask the caller: Are you needing to schedule an appointment with a new primary care provider?

  2. If Yes, proceed to Step 2.

  3. If the caller is currently an existing patient:

    1. Inform the caller: This number is for scheduling new patients.

    2. Ask the caller: Are you interested in scheduling with a new provider, or do you want to stay with your current provider?

    3. If the patient wants to schedule with their current provider:

      1. Note in the Service Order the caller is not a new patient.

      2. Determine if the practice is open and if so, warm transfer the caller.

      3. See Article 5814 for instructions.

      4. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

    4. If the patient would like to schedule with a new provider, proceed to Step 2.

docs.title: Step 2: Gather Patient's Insurance and Determine if Accepted
docs.text:
1. See Article 6271 to determine if the caller's insurance is accepted.

  2. Note the caller's insurance.

  3. If accepted:

    1. Inform the patient: Your insurance is accepted.

    2. Proceed to Step 3.

  4. If NOT accepted, ask the caller: Your insurance is not currently accepted. Would you still like to schedule a new appointment?

    1. If Yes, proceed to Step 3.

    2. If No:

      1. Inform the patient: Thank you for calling. Have a great day.

      2. Close the Service Order.

  5. If the patient does not have insurance, proceed to Step 3.

docs.title: Step 3: Determine which City or Town the Patient Needs to Schedule In
docs.text:
1. Inform the caller of the available service area locations.

  2. Ask the caller: Which area do you live closest to, or where would you like to see the provider?

  3. Note the caller's response.

docs.title: Step 4: Determine which Practice to Schedule With
docs.text:
1. In a separate browser tab, navigate to the PCPs Taking New Patients & Online Scheduling reference spreadsheet.

  2. At the bottom, click on the PCPs Taking New Patients tab.

    1. The practices are the colored rows with the PCPs taking new patients listed below.

    2. Practices are grouped by city, with each city being a separate color.

  3. In column A [City / Town], locate the city or town noted by the caller in Step 3.

  4. Provide the caller the name of the practices and their location listed for the city or town to determine which the caller would like an appointment at.

    1. The practice's name is in column B [Practice / Provider].

    2. The address and location are on the same line to the right.

    3. ⚠️ The practice must have providers listed below it to be able to schedule a new patient.

  5. If you are unable to determine what practice to schedule the caller at:

    1. If current time is Monday–Friday, 8am–4:30pm:

      1. Warm transfer the caller to the Scheduling Supervisor.

      2. Close the Service Order.

    2. If outside standard hours or the warm transfer is unsuccessful:

      1. Proceed to Step 8.

  6. Note the selected practice in the Service Order.

docs.title: Step 5: Determine which Provider to Schedule With
docs.text:
1. If the caller requests a specific provider, try to schedule with that provider first.

  2. Review the providers listed under the practice selected in Step 4.

    1. If more than one provider is listed, check in column G [Scheduling Priority] if one should be picked first.

    2. If no priority is listed, any provider listed can be selected.

  3. Verify in column F [Age / Insurance] that the patient's age and insurance noted in Step 2 are accepted.

  4. In column H [Special Instructions], check for any information noted and provide it to the caller.

  5. If the caller requests information about the provider, a link to the provider's biographical information is available in column E [Provider Bio Link].

  6. Note the selected provider in the Service Order.

  7. Proceed to Step 6.

docs.title: Step 6: Select an Appointment with the Provider
docs.text:
1. For the selected provider, in column D [Online Scheduling Link], click the scheduling link to pull up the provider's available appointments.

    1. If the message "Sorry, we couldn't find any open appointments." appears:

      1. Repeat Step 5 to see if there are additional providers listed for the same practice.

      2. If no additional providers are listed for the practice, ask the caller: Would you like to schedule an appointment at a different location?

        1. If Yes:

          1. Repeat Steps 4.1–4.4 to determine if another practice is acceptable for the caller.

          2. If another practice is found, repeat Step 5.

        2. If No:

          1. Note the following in the Service Order:

            1. The practice the patient needs scheduled at.

            2. Dates and times the caller needs.

          2. Determine if the practice is open and if so, warm transfer the caller.

          3. See Article 5814 for instructions.

          4. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

  2. Review the appointments with the caller to determine which appointment they would like.

  3. If "more..." is below an appointment time, hover your mouse over it to view additional times available.

  4. If an appointment is not available within 7 days:

    1. Repeat Step 5 to see if there are additional providers listed for the same practice.

    2. If no additional providers have availability at the same practice within 7 days, inform the caller of the next available appointment.

      1. If the next available appointment is acceptable, proceed to Step 6.4.

      2. If the next available appointment is NOT acceptable, ask the caller: Would you like to schedule an appointment at a different location?

        1. If Yes:

          1. Repeat Steps 4.3–4.7 to determine if another practice is acceptable for the caller.

          2. If another practice is found, repeat Step 5.

        2. If No:

          1. If the next available appointment is more than 2 weeks out, ask the caller:

            Would you like to schedule the appointment and be put on a waitlist if a sooner appointment opens up?

            If a sooner appointment does become available, you'll receive a text notification to your cell phone notifying you.

            1. If Yes:

              1. Note the following in the Service Order:

                1. "SMS Waitlist opt-in"

                2. Cellphone number.

              2. Proceed to Step 6.4.

            2. If No:

              1. Note the following in the Service Order:

                1. The practice the patient needs scheduled at.

                2. Dates and times the caller needs.

              2. Determine if the practice is open and if so, warm transfer the caller.

              3. See Article 5814 for instructions.

              4. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

          2. If the next available appointment is less than 2 weeks out:

            1. Note the following in the Service Order:

              1. The practice the patient needs scheduled at.

              2. Dates and times the caller needs.

            2. Determine if the practice is open and if so, warm transfer the caller.

            3. See Article 5814 for instructions.

            4. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

  5. Proceed to Step 7.

docs.title: Step 7: Gather Patient Information and Schedule Appointment
docs.text:
1. Click the selected appointment.

  2. In the pop-up, complete the CAPTCHA.

  3. Verify the provider, appointment date and time, and location listed are correct.

  4. In the "Reason for Visit" field, enter the caller's reason.

    1. For example, "New patient visit", or if the caller mentions any additional information, enter that as well.

  5. Click Continue.

  6. On the right, under "Continue as a Guest", click Continue.

  7. On the Patient Information screen, ask for and enter the patient's information.

    1. Items listed with red asterisks (*) are required.

    2. ⚠️ Do NOT request the patient's Social Security Number!

      1. For the Social Security Number field, enter either "000-00-0000" or "999-99-9999".

    3. Click Next.

  8. On the Insurance Information screen, ask for and enter the patient's insurance information.

    1. This information can be found on their insurance card for the insurance noted in Step 2.

    2. If the caller does not have insurance, select No Insurance.

    3. If the caller's insurance was not listed in Article 6271, select Not Listed.

    4. Items listed with red asterisks (*) are required.

    5. FYI: "Subscriber ID" may appear as "Subscriber Number", "Member Number", "Member ID", "Policy Number", "Policy ID", etc.

    6. Click Schedule it! to complete the appointment.

  9. Inform the caller: You will shortly receive an e-mail confirmation of your appointment, as well as an e-mail containing a link for your patient portal registration.

  10. If the caller's insurance was not listed in Article 6271:

    1. Provide the phone number to the office and offer a warm transfer if they would like to speak to someone about their insurance.

    2. If needed, see Article 5814 for instructions to warm transfer the caller.

  11. If the appointment is successfully scheduled:

    1. Note the following in the Service Order:

      1. "Appointment Scheduled"

      2. Appointment Date.

      3. Appointment Time.

      4. Appointment Location.

    2. Close the Service Order.

  12. If unable to schedule the appointment:

    1. Note the following in the Service Order:

      1. The practice the patient needs scheduled at.

      2. Dates and times the caller needs.

      3. Any errors or issues.

    2. Determine if the practice is open and if so, warm transfer the caller.

    3. See Article 5814 for instructions.

    4. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

docs.title: Step 8: Escalation
docs.text:
1. If the issue remains unresolved, follow the appropriate escalation procedure.

  2. See Article 3956 for instructions.
